issue.title: Select Android ROS integration path (rosbridge vs ros2_java) and document decision

comment.body:
@Ma7moud3ly thanks for putting this comparison together.

A few clarifying questions, mainly because I think some of the wording may be assuming a different hardware/deployment model than this project actually has.

Our robot is an Android phone plus an RP2040 MCU driving the wheels. The ROS system itself would be running on a nearby PC, not on the robot hardware directly. With that in mind, I’m not sure I’m interpreting some parts of the comparison correctly:

- When you describe `rosbridge` as robot-side and reference `sudo apt install ros-$DISTRO-rosbridge-suite`, is the assumption that `rosbridge` would run on the nearby ROS PC rather than on the phone/RP2040 hardware?

A few other points I’d like clarified:

- You describe `rosbridge` as Quality Level 3. Can you link the definition you’re using for that and explain what practical conclusion you want us to draw from it here?
- You mention message types and rates above, and then give a 5–20 ms latency figure on local Wi-Fi. Which specific message types/rates are you referring to, and was that figure measured for this project or taken from external documentation?
- More generally, is the proposed architecture that the Android phone would connect to an external ROS system running on a nearby PC, or is some other deployment model intended? I think that distinction matters quite a bit for evaluating these options fairly.

I think the conclusion may still be reasonable, but I’d like to make sure the comparison matches the actual hardware/setup we’re targeting.
